summary_text: Most deserves implementation now: **fix exit-code semantics between `fugue-lane-bridge` and `fugue-execute`**. `fugue-lane-bridge` returns `1` when required lanes fail ([fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L789)), but `fugue-execute` treats any non-`2` as OK ([fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L390)). Then Phase 2 gates only check `result.ok` ([phase-runner.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/phase-runner.mjs#L223), [phase-runner.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/phase-runner.mjs#L236), [phase-runner.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/phase-runner.mjs#L286)), so partial lane failure can silently pass planning/simulation/implementation. This is a direct production correctness risk and currently untested. Compared residuals: - **Run-scoped phase evidence**: real risk, but currently half-dormant. Guard checks phase evidence only when `matrix.tier >= 3` ([fugue-lane-bridge.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs#L694)), yet Phase 2 matrices do not include `tier` ([fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L442), [fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L459), [fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L483)). Also evidence validation is global and not run-scoped ([guard.js](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/guard.js#L104)). Important, but second priority after exit-code mismatch. - **Tier-policy source-of-truth drift**: medium risk. Tier is inferred heuristically ([fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L280)) and gate requirements can be recipe-overridden ([phase-runner.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/phase-runner.mjs#L212), [recipe-loader.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/lib/recipe-loader.mjs#L166)); this is governance inconsistency, but less immediately dangerous than silent partial-failure acceptance. - **Workflow violation to call out**: Tier 2 allows `min_lanes: 1` ([fugue-execute.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/fugue-execute.mjs#L484)), i.e., single-lane fallback in a structured multi-lane phase. Test coverage gap remains for the top risk: current Phase 2 tests cover success/security-veto/vote-blocked, but not bridge exit `1` partial-failure propagation ([test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-canon-v4-smoke.mjs#L72), [test-phase2-state-recording.mjs](/tmp/phase0-1c8023/assets/skills/orchestra-delegator/scripts/tests/test-phase2-state-recording.mjs#L86)).
